title: WorldEvent
description: Atomic unit of observation in CoreMind. Produced by L1 plugins and consumed by the L2 World Model.
$comment: additionalProperties is intentionally omitted (defaults to true) per §5.4 forward-compatibility: consumers must silently ignore unknown fields. Do not add additionalProperties: false — it would reject valid events from newer plugin versions.

id.description: Globally unique, time-sortable ULID identifier. Enables deduplication and time-ordered storage without a separate timestamp index.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp with millisecond precision, Z suffix. The moment of observation, not ingest time.

source.description: Stable reverse-domain plugin identifier matching the id declared in the plugin's PluginManifest.

source_version.description: Semver version of the emitting plugin at the time of emission. Used for audit traceability and compatibility gating.

signature.description: ed25519 signature of the RFC 8785 canonical form of this event (signature field excluded). Format: ed25519:<128-char lowercase hex>.

attribute.description: Lowercase dot-notation name for the observed property of the entity. Must be stable across plugin MAJOR versions.

value.description: The observed value. May be any JSON type including null. null indicates a previously known value is no longer observed.

confidence.description: Probability that the observation is accurate, in the range [0.0, 1.0]. Use 1.0 for deterministic sensors.

unit.description: SI or conventional unit for a numeric value, e.g. °C, kWh, USD, lux, %. Omit for dimensionless or non-numeric values.

EntityRef.description: Reference to the entity being observed. The combined key type/id uniquely identifies an entity across all plugins and all time.

type.description: Entity class. Canonical values: room, person, device, project, task, transaction, email, calendar_event, document. New types require an RFC.

id.description: Stable identifier within the type namespace, preferably the upstream system's native identifier.

Delta.description: Change since the immediately preceding event for this (entity, attribute) pair. Recommended for quantitative attributes.

absolute.description: current_value minus previous_value. Negative indicates a decrease. Meaningful only for numeric values.

relative_pct.description: (absolute / abs(previous_value)) × 100. Must be omitted when previous_value is 0 to avoid division by zero.

previous_value.description: The value from the immediately preceding event for this (entity, attribute). May be any JSON type, matching the type of value.

Context.description: Supporting context for the reasoning layers. All sub-fields are optional.

trend_window.description: Duration over which trend_direction was computed, e.g. 1h, 24h, 7d.

trend_direction.description: Trend direction over trend_window. One of: rising, falling, stable, volatile.

related_entities.description: Other entities causally or contextually related to this event. Used by L2 to maintain relationship edges.

tags.description: Lowercase hyphen-separated labels for filtering and coarse categorisation.
